data.narrative:
Kreios Space is a seed-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised €8M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 data points across the sector.

Standard triangulation methods proved inapplicable to this valuation. Peer-median analysis cannot be executed: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar valuation tier) yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples is also unavailable; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S stands at 49.8x across the 6 public tickers, but Kreios Space's revenue is not disclosed—standard practice for private companies at seed stage. Without either sufficient narrow-stage peer benchmarks or disclosed revenue, conventional cross-sectional methods cannot be reliably applied.

This analysis underscores the structural constraint of seed-stage aerospace valuations: the sector supports robust public comps and an identifiable private peer universe, yet the combination of sparse early-stage data density and absent revenue disclosure prevents quantitative triangulation. A credible valuation would require either (1) disclosure of revenue or revenue projections, (2) identification of additional same-stage comparables with known terms, or (3) reliance on alternative methods such as venture-capital method or founder/investor track record analysis—none of which are supported by the current dataset.
